channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: St. Patrick's Day parade celebrates Boston heritage in America’s most Irish big city
item.description: It's that time of year again when more than a million green-clad revelers fill the streets of America’s most Irish big city for the South Boston St. Patrick’s Day parade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: At least 59 dead and more than 150 injured in nightclub fire in North Macedonia
item.description: KOCANI, North Macedonia (AP) — A massive fire tore through a nightclub in North Macedonia ’s eastern town of Kocani early Sunday, killing 59 people and injuring 155, authorities said. The blaze broke out around 2:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: BORIS GRDANOSKI and KONSTANTIN TESTORIDES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 59 dead, more than 150 injured in nightclub fire in North Macedonia
item.description: A massive fire tore through a nightclub in North Macedonia’s eastern town of Kocani early Sunday, killing 59 people and injuring 155, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney seeks alliances in Europe as he deals with Trump
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — New Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney is heading to Paris and London on Monday to seek alliances as he deals with U.S. President Donald Trump's attacks on Canada's sovereignty and economy.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look at some of the worst fires at nightclubs and music venues
item.description: A fire at a nightclub in the town of Kocani in North Macedonia has killed 59 people and injured more than 150 others, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Angst pervades a pair of Republican town halls
item.description: In two congressional districts and vastly different political environments, two Republican congressmen were met with far different reactions at public meetings they held late last week.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Makiya Seminera And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McLaren's Lando Norris wins wet and wild Australian Grand Prix. Hamilton finishes 10th
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — McLaren’s Lando Norris won a chaotic rain-affected Australian Grand Prix, his first victory at Albert Park, with the British driver just managing to stay ahead of Red Bull’s Max Verstappen following a third safety car late in Sunday's season-opening Formula 1 race.
item.dc:creator: STEWART BELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, KTLA 5

item.title: Bulls title banners removed for season after sustaining damage during Disturbed concert
item.description: All six Bulls championship banners have been removed from the United Center for the rest of the season after sustaining damage during a concert.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks threat of tornadoes and fire, killing at least 16
item.description: The threat of violent tornadoes in parts of the U.S. proved deadly as well as destructive as whipping winds moved east into the Mississippi Valley and Deep South on Saturday, with at least 16 people killed and scores of homes decimated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 32 dead in massive US storm after new fatalities reported in Kansas and Mississippi
item.description: At least 32 people have been killed in a monster storm sweeping across much of the U.S. Kansas authorities said eight people died in a highway pileup caused by a dust storm Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Russ Bynum And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration brushed aside decades of precedent when it ordered Columbia University to oust the leadership of an academic department, a demand seen as a direct attack on academic freedom and a warning of what’s to come for other colleges facing federal scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, KRON4

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USPS agrees to work with DOGE on reform, planning to cut 10,000 workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Postmaster General Louis DeJoy plans to cut 10,000 workers and billions of dollars from the U.S. Postal Service budget and he’ll do that working with Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency, according to a letter sent to members of Congress on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: The Trump administration has moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma for their immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DHS revokes visa of one Columbia student, arrests another pro-Palestinian protester
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) announced Friday it has revoked the visa of one Columbia University student and arrested another person involved in the pro-Palestinian protests, escalating its crackdown on those engaged in demonstrations over the conflict in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)] funding is now forcing us to wind down critical work here in Baltimore and internationally,” a spokesperson for the school said in an email.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Indiana officer gets unpaid leave after allegedly leaving child out in cold
item.description: Indiana police said a child in officer Cody Scott’s care was forced to stand outside in frigid temperatures with a sign that read, in part, “I want to stab and kill my brother.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Dreasler, NewsNation

item.title: The Justice Department is investigating whether Columbia University hid students sought by the US
item.description: A top U.S. Justice Department official says the department is investigating whether Columbia University concealed “illegal aliens” on its campus.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Some universities are freezing hiring and laying off staff as Trump cuts federal funding
item.description: Universities across the U.S. have announced hiring freezes, citing new financial uncertainty as the Trump administration threatens a range of cuts to federal contracts and research grants. Some have announced layoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to partly allow birthright citizenship restrictions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is asking the Supreme Court to allow restrictions on birthright citizenship to partly take effect while legal fights play out.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dem AGs sue over Trump administration’s Education Department layoffs
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 20 states sued Thursday over the Trump administration’s efforts to lay off nearly half of the Education Department workforce.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices, but the US is hesitant
item.description: Without a new policy including vaccines, the government will continue to slaughter every flock with a bird flu infection to limit the spread of the disease. Those deaths have totaled over 166 million birds in the U.S. since 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Strip hotel to be redesigned by reality TV star
item.description: Lisa Vanderpump announced Wednesday she plans to completely renovate a Strip hotel, transforming it into the "Vanderpump Hotel.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, ABC 4
